expectations.text: recommendations.md discusses naming with explicit reference to grep-ability, retrieval, or localization
expectations.evidence: Section 4 says 'Agents grep for createUser and find it in utils.ts... They edit the wrong one.'

expectations.text: recommendations.md recommends organizing code by feature / vertical slice rather than horizontal layer
expectations.evidence: Section 3 advocates flat feature files and keeping route, validation, handler, and types within 1-2 files.

expectations.text: recommendations.md warns against metaprogramming, reflection, or dynamic dispatch
expectations.evidence: Section 17 'Avoid magic metaprogramming' warns against decorators, Proxy-based ORMs, and plugin transforms.

expectations.text: recommendations.md recommends typed public boundaries / type annotations at module seams
expectations.evidence: Section 2 'Make the type system the source of truth' plus Section 16 JSDoc on public APIs.

expectations.text: recommendations.md recommends verification affordances tied to preventing agent hallucination spirals
expectations.evidence: Section 12 mentions one validation command but does not tie verification to hallucination spirals or wrong-patch loops.

expectations.text: recommendations.md addresses file size, near-duplicates, or str_replace breakage
expectations.evidence: Section 8 targets 5-40 line functions and flags 300-line functions as a failure mode, though does not mention str_replace.

expectations.text: recommendations.md grounds reasoning in specific agent failure modes rather than generic clean-code platitudes
expectations.evidence: Failure modes are mostly generic ('agent picks one uniformly', 'agent will invent'); lacks concrete context window, str_replace, lost-in-the-middle, or documented spiral citations.

expectations.text: recommendations.md presents the list in a prioritized order
expectations.evidence: Explicit P0/P1/P2 legend with sections ordered accordingly.

expectations.text: recommendations.md covers deterministic tests (seeded randomness, frozen time) OR injectable side effects — v1.3 guidance
expectations.evidence: Section 19 prescribes seeded randomness, fixture factories, and injecting a clock instead of Date.now().

expectations.text: recommendations.md mentions branded/nominal types OR discriminated unions over magic strings — v1.3 guidance
expectations.evidence: Section 22 'Make failure states first-class' uses discriminated union {ok:true} | {ok:false} pattern; Section 7 uses exhaustive switches on unions.

expectations.text: recommendations.md flags barrel re-export files or 'export *' as an anti-pattern — v1.3 guidance (AR011)
expectations.evidence: Section 4 explicitly advises avoiding barrel index.ts re-exports across the whole app.

expectations.text: recommendations.md mentions dependency pinning OR training-data / 'boring stack' considerations — v1.3 guidance
expectations.evidence: Section 11 'Commit lockfiles' covers dependency pinning via lockfiles.
